steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The student’s claim hinges on the idea that current methods have a lineage that long predates the completion of Lake Sabrina in 1908.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D cites the Hohokam irrigation system from the 7th century CE, showing that design principles used today trace back centuries before Lake Sabrina, directly confirming the claimed long lineage.
The other choices either discuss only modern techniques (A, C) or stress present‑day importance without historical depth (B), so they do not support the idea of a pre‑1908 lineage.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
